book_claims.claim: Zucchini is a frequently used vegetable in the Longevity Diet, appearing across multiple dishes including rice with zucchini and peas, Ligurian minestrone, and black rice with shrimp.
book_claims.mechanism: Low-calorie, high-water vegetable that contributes volume and micronutrients to meals without adding significant caloric load; supports satiety on a calorie-appropriate longevity diet.

supplementary_research.finding: PMID 30263869: High vegetable consumption (≥5 servings/day including squash varieties) is associated with a 26% reduction in all-cause mortality; mechanistically linked to antioxidant phytochemicals, fiber, and potassium.

PMID 29099553: Cucurbit vegetables including zucchini contain cucurbitacins and carotenoids (lutein, zeaxanthin) with demonstrated anti-inflammatory and anti-proliferative properties in vitro, consistent with longevity-promoting dietary patterns.

Examine confirms zucchini is a source of vitamin C (~18 mg/100 g), potassium (~262 mg/100 g), folate (~24 mcg/100 g), and lutein + zeaxanthin (~2.1 mg/100 g); the lutein content supports eye and brain health in aging.

culinary_pairings.source: Rice with zucchini dish in The Longevity Diet

Standard cooking fat for sautéed or grilled zucchini

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Supports blood pressure regulation and cardiovascular health; retained well in steaming vs boiling.
Water-soluble; light steaming or sautéing preserves most vitamin C; boiling can reduce by 30–50%.
Contributes to daily folate intake; important for DNA methylation and cell division.
Fat-soluble carotenoids that protect the macula and support brain health; bioavailability enhanced by cooking with olive oil.

synergies.description: Featured together in the Longevity Diet rice with zucchini recipe; zucchini adds hydration, vitamin C, and potassium while wild rice provides sustained energy and complete grain nutrition.
Sautéing zucchini in olive oil increases absorption of fat-soluble carotenoids (lutein, zeaxanthin, beta-carotene); the standard Mediterranean preparation method.
Paired in the Longevity Diet black rice with shrimp and zucchini dish; shrimp provides omega-3 and iodine while zucchini contributes fiber, potassium, and phytonutrients.
Classic Ligurian minestrone pairing; basil's volatile phenolics (linalool, eugenol) add anti-inflammatory compounds to the micronutrient-rich zucchini base.
